mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the t-test of mean returns against zero yields p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate borrowing costs and reduces present values of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal a broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or an interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during late-cycle expansions signals capacity utilization pressures that widen profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO during late-cycle months coincide with negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals underlying aggregate demand strength that supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to exhibit a positive rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) concurrent with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals monetary restriction into a weakening economy, which compresses earnings multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Annual consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 4.0% signals erosion of real corporate earnings and impending Federal Reserve tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 4.0%, over any 3-year rolling window.
